outlets.name: CNN
outlets.headline: Oil prices rise as Iran doubles down on Strait of Hormuz closure; IDF strikes Beqaa Valley
outlets.summary: CNN leads with economic angle — oil surging to $108+ as Iran's Deputy Speaker declares Hormuz closure permanent. IDF Beqaa Valley strikes first in 3 weeks. Khamenei 'martyr' mural deepens mystery. Goldman raises forecasts. War Powers deadline approaching.

outlets.name: NBC News
outlets.headline: Trump is facing an increasingly patient Iran as oil prices spike and peace talks stall
outlets.summary: NBC frames Iran as outlasting US pressure — analytical lens suggesting Tehran's patience strategy may be working. Oil spike to $107.97 underscores economic costs of stalemate. Iran's new proposal positions Tehran as reasonable party.

outlets.name: Fox News
outlets.headline: Trump to hold Situation Room meeting on Iran's Hormuz offer; Hegseth: blockade 'growing and going global'
outlets.summary: Fox News frames US as dominant — Hegseth: 'no one sails without US Navy permission.' Trump NSC meeting Monday to review Iran proposal. Iran's internal contradictions noted: proposal to reopen vs Nikzad's 'under no circumstances.' IDF strikes Beqaa Valley.

outlets.name: Daily Wire
outlets.headline: Israel secretly deployed Iron Dome to UAE — first foreign deployment
outlets.summary: Right-leaning outlets emphasize Israel-UAE military cooperation as vindication of Abraham Accords legacy. Iron Dome success story. Bennett-Lapid alliance seen as political threat to Netanyahu.

outlets.name: Al Jazeera
outlets.headline: 'Excessive US demands' led to failure of talks — Araghchi blames US as IDF strikes Beqaa Valley
outlets.summary: Al Jazeera leads with Araghchi blaming 'excessive US demands' for failed talks. IDF strikes Beqaa Valley first in 3 weeks — ceasefire near collapse. Nikzad's Hormuz 'under no circumstances' statement contradicts Iran's own reopening proposal. Lebanon ceasefire deteriorating.

outlets.name: BBC
outlets.headline: King Charles arrives in Washington for state visit as Iran war dominates agenda
outlets.summary: BBC frames King Charles visit through prism of UK-US relations strained by Iran war. 29 MPs called for cancellation. Trump-Starmer Hormuz call signals UK-France initiative on freedom of navigation.

outlets.name: Times of Israel
outlets.headline: IDF strikes Hezbollah sites in Beqaa Valley for first time in 3 weeks; weapons cache found in children's bedroom
outlets.summary: Times of Israel leads with IDF Beqaa Valley escalation — first strikes there in 3 weeks. Weapons cache in children's bedroom underscores Hezbollah tactics. Ceasefire near total collapse after Fooks KIA. Nikzad: Hormuz 'under no circumstances' returns.

outlets.name: Haaretz
outlets.headline: Report: Israel sent Iron Dome battery, troops to help defend UAE during Iran war
outlets.summary: Haaretz emphasizes the unprecedented Israel-Gulf military cooperation. Analysis frames this as new era of Middle East security architecture. Concern about provoking further Iranian escalation against Gulf states.

outlets.name: Press TV
outlets.headline: Hezbollah vows 'legitimate response' to Israeli ceasefire violations exceeding 500
outlets.summary: Press TV leads with Hezbollah's defiance narrative — 500+ Israeli violations cited. IDF Beqaa Valley strikes framed as Israeli aggression. Iran's Nikzad declares Hormuz permanent. Khamenei mural treated as tribute rather than death speculation.

outlets.name: Tehran Times
outlets.headline: Iran proposes Hormuz reopening — nuclear program talks to follow in separate track
outlets.summary: Iranian media frames the new proposal as Iran taking the initiative for peace while US remains bellicose. Blockade framed as the primary obstacle. Commercial flight resumption highlighted as victory narrative.
